revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Wikisource author-inline/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}}
===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }}
===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage.
== See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}}
==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}}
===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }}
===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage.
== See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}}
==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}}
===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }}
===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage.
== See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}}
==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] }}</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}}
===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }}
===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage.
== See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}}
==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}} ===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }} ===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}} ==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- CATEGORIES HERE, THANKS --> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}} ===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }} ===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}} ==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD TEMPLATE CATEGORIES AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> {{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}} ===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }} ===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}} ==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}}
{{Lua|Module:WikidataIB}}
This template when used on an author's page will utilise interwiki links stored at Wikidata to create the link and the label to the author page at [[s:|English Wikisource]]
== Usage == For adding inline to "External links" listing.
===Standard usage===
In most cases it will be used ''without'' parameters At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline}}</syntaxhighlight> produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare}} ===With "list" parameter===
The template can also be used to produce a list of works by a particular author:
At [[:en:William Shakespeare|William Shakespeare]] <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{Wikisource author-inline|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|]]'' – 1598 }}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{Wikisource author-inline|William Shakespeare|list= * ''[[s:All's Well That Ends Well|All's Well That Ends Well]]'' – 1598 * ''[[s:As You Like It|As You Like It]]'' – 1598 }} ===Overriding parameters=== To note that parameters can still be utilised to override the presentation, though this would not be the preferred means as when a page is moved here or at Wikisource the link would be broken. An example of such usage would be adding an author link on an article that is a list of works of the author.
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext">{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}</syntaxhighlight>
produces:
{{wikisource author-inline|wslink=Alice Bertha Gomme|title=Alice Gomme}}
"list" parameter can be applied with override parameters though that would be an unusual usage. == See also == * {{tl|Wikisource author}} * {{tl|Wikisource-inline}} ==Maintenance== Templates applied to articles without Wikidata items will be listed in [[:Category:Wikisource templates with missing id]].
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other||
}}</includeonly> <noinclude> [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates using data from Wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
